_comment_top: Hiveboard MCP server — example client configurations.
The server is hosted alongside the REST API at /mcp using HTTP/SSE transport.
All requests require X-Api-Key (admin/coordinator key for control-plane actions,
or any agent API key for worker / orchestrator flows).
Replace HIVEBOARD_BASE_URL with your deployment (e.g. https://hiveboard.example.com).
Replace HIVEBOARD_API_KEY with the plaintext key issued by Hiveboard.
Tools exposed (PRD §5.4):
hiveboard_list_tasks       — list project tasks with optional filters
hiveboard_get_task         — full task context bundle
hiveboard_update_status    — transition task status
hiveboard_add_note         — add a note to a task
hiveboard_decompose_task   — break a task into 1-50 subtasks
hiveboard_add_decision     — record an architectural decision
hiveboard_get_dependencies — project dependency graph
hiveboard_my_tasks         — calling agent's tasks + notification count
hiveboard_get_notifications — calling agent's unread notifications
Resources exposed (PRD §5.4):
hiveboard://project/{id}/overview   — project summary + stats
hiveboard://task/{id}/context       — full task context bundle
hiveboard://project/{id}/decisions  — all decision records

githubCopilot._comment: .vscode/mcp.json — GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code

_comment: If your client only supports stdio MCP, run the official mcp-remote bridge
and let the API key flow via the HIVEBOARD_API_KEY environment variable.
(The Hiveboard server itself reads HIVEBOARD_API_KEY when an X-Api-Key header
is missing on /mcp requests — useful for the same self-hosted bridge case.)
